meta.title: ASM Withdrawal — Individualised Recurrence & Long-term Outcome (Lamberink)
meta.authors: Lamberink HJ, Otte WM, Geerts AT, et al.
meta.outcome: Individualised risk of seizure recurrence (at 2 and 5 years) and the chance of long-term seizure freedom (at 10 years) after withdrawal of antiseizure medication in a seizure-free patient, from two published nomograms.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. This reproduces the two individualised nomograms of Lamberink et al. (Lancet Neurology 2017), an individual-participant-data meta-analysis of 1,769 seizure-free patients who withdrew antiseizure medication (ASM). It estimates, for one patient, both the risk of seizure recurrence (at 2 and 5 years) and the chance of long-term seizure freedom (at 10 years).

How to read it

  • Enter the patient's clinical details; the tool returns the 2-year and 5-year recurrence risk and the 10-year chance of long-term seizure freedom.
  • The strongest predictors of recurrence are a longer duration of epilepsy, a shorter seizure-free interval, and the absence of a self-limiting epilepsy syndrome; a longer seizure-free interval is protective.
  • Note the two outcomes use different predictor sets — e.g. number of ASMs, sex, family history and focal seizures affect only the long-term outcome; age at onset, febrile seizures, self-limiting syndrome and developmental delay affect only recurrence.

Key facts

  • Adjusted c-statistic 0.65 for recurrence and 0.71 for long-term seizure freedom, with stable internal–external cross-validation across 10 cohorts.
  • Overall, ~46% of patients had a recurrence; ~80% of those regained seizure control on restarting medication.
  • The 2-year rule is only a threshold — every additional seizure-free year further lowers the recurrence risk; the nomogram helps individualise the timing (relevant to driving/COSY decisions).

Reproduced exactly from the authors' open-source implementation (epilepsypredictiontools.info); verified against the paper's worked examples (child → 28%/36% recurrence; the female example → 97% long-term freedom). Demonstration and counseling aid — not a substitute for specialist judgment.

predictors.name: Epilepsy duration before remission
predictors.assess: Years from the first to the last seizure before the patient became seizure-free. Longer active epilepsy raises recurrence risk (and lowers long-term freedom); the effect plateaus beyond ~8 years.

predictors.name: Seizure-free interval before withdrawal
predictors.assess: Years the patient has been seizure-free at the point of ASM withdrawal. A longer seizure-free interval strongly lowers recurrence risk — each extra seizure-free year reduces the risk.

predictors.name: Number of ASMs at withdrawal
predictors.assess: Number of antiseizure medications the patient is taking at the time of withdrawal. Only affects the long-term seizure-freedom outcome; more drugs lowers the chance of long-term freedom.

predictors.name: Age at onset of epilepsy
predictors.assess: Age (years) at epilepsy onset. The relationship with recurrence is U-shaped: risk is lowest around ages 3–10 and rises for very early or later onset. Only affects the recurrence outcome.

predictors.name: Sex
predictors.assess: Patient sex. Female sex slightly lowers the chance of long-term seizure freedom (long-term outcome only).

predictors.name: Family history of epilepsy
predictors.assess: Epilepsy in a first- or second-degree relative. A positive family history lowers the chance of long-term freedom (long-term outcome only).

predictors.name: History of febrile seizures
predictors.assess: Any history of febrile seizures. Raises recurrence risk (recurrence outcome only).

predictors.name: Number of seizures before remission
predictors.assess: Total number of seizures the patient ever had before achieving remission. Ten or more raises recurrence risk and lowers long-term freedom.

predictors.name: Self-limiting epilepsy syndrome
predictors.assess: A self-limiting (formerly 'benign') epilepsy syndrome — e.g. childhood absence, Rolandic (centrotemporal-spike) or Panayiotopoulos syndrome. Its ABSENCE markedly raises recurrence risk (+5.5 points); recurrence outcome only.

predictors.name: Developmental delay (IQ < 70)
predictors.assess: Developmental delay / intellectual disability (IQ below 70, or by clinical judgment). Raises recurrence risk (recurrence outcome only).

predictors.name: Focal seizures
predictors.assess: Presence of focal (partial-onset) seizures. Lowers the chance of long-term seizure freedom (long-term outcome only).

predictors.name: EEG before withdrawal
predictors.assess: EEG findings before ASM withdrawal. Epileptiform abnormality raises recurrence risk and lowers long-term freedom; a normal or not-performed EEG adds no points.

model.note: Two separate nomogram scores. Recurrence risk (2 & 5 yr) and long-term seizure freedom (10 yr) use different predictor subsets. Population-level estimates — complement, do not replace, clinical judgment.
model.viz_info: Each factor adds points to one or both nomogram scores (recurrence and/or long-term freedom). The two totals are looked up in the published risk tables to give the recurrence risk at 2 and 5 years and the chance of long-term seizure freedom at 10 years.

provenance.source: Lamberink et al., Lancet Neurol 2017;16(6):523-531 (doi 10.1016/S1474-4422(17)30114-X, PMID 28483337), Figures 2A & 3A nomograms. Exact point lookup tables + risk tables taken from the authors' open-source web tool epilepsypredictiontools.info (js/aed-calc.js, ported from their function_aed.php; repo github.com/wmotte/epilepsypredictiontools).
provenance.notes: Two-nomogram multiscore. Recurrence total (r) = duration + seizure-free-interval + age-onset + febrile + n-seizures + self-limiting-absent(5.5) + developmental-delay + EEG; looked up in RISK2Y/RISK5Y (index=round(total*2)). Long-term total (l) = duration + seizure-free-interval + n-ASMs + sex + family-history + n-seizures + focal + EEG; looked up in RISK_LONG. All point arrays value-indexed from aed-calc.js; continuous predictors (duration 0-40, ttr 0-24, ageonset 0-80 [non-monotonic U-shape], naed 0-9) carry per-value lookup arrays pts_r/pts_l. VERIFIED against paper worked examples: child (onset3/dur1/ttr2/febNeg/<10sz/not-self-limiting/no-delay/normalEEG) → recurrence total 27.5 → 28% (2y) / 36% (5y) EXACT; female (dur1/ttr2/1ASM/female/famNeg/<10sz/no-focal/normalEEG) → long total 19.5 → 97% freedom EXACT. c-stat 0.65 recurrence / 0.71 long-term. Complements form28 (Lamberink EEG-stratified COSY curves) with the individualised nomogram. Extracted 2026-07-08.
